_purpose: Canonical 3D-scene vantages for the visual-regression + perf sweeps (scripts/probe-sweep.js). This is the golden spec: each vantage teleports the probe to a fixed camera + time/sea, captures one screenshot, and records fps. The `expect` text is what a correct frame looks like; `regressions` lists the specific failures this vantage exists to catch (each has bitten a real release). Data-driven per the infinite-of-x rule - add a vantage here, both sweeps pick it up. Coordinates are the same debug/camera_request.json + debug/showcase_request.json fields lib.rs polls.
_perf_note: perf_floor_fps is an advisory lower bound at this vantage on the dev machine (RTX 4070). The perf sweep flags a stop below it; it is a smoke threshold, not a hard gate.

vantages.desc: Ground level in the Sahara at local noon, facing the horizon
vantages.expect: A deep, saturated blue sky dome fading to a pale warm band at the horizon, over rolling tan sand. Bright and clearly daytime.
regressions: NO stars visible (v0.913-era daytime star bleed)
no washed-out near-white dome (v0.918 exposure)
no hard-edged black shell or ring around the horizon

vantages.desc: 8 m above a calm sea, grazing angle to the horizon
vantages.expect: Deep rippled blue water reading as gentle rolling swell all the way to a clean horizon, under a blue-to-warm sky. Fine surface structure, not glassy-flat.
regressions: NO black/white zebra banding (v0.921 analytic-wave aliasing)
no concentric moire interference rings
no white-out sheet at the grazing angle

vantages.desc: 6 m above a storm sea, grazing angle
vantages.expect: Dark blue-grey choppy water with organic white foam streaks riding the wave crests (roughly a third coverage at most). Reads as a rough sea, not a white sheet.
regressions: no full-white ocean (v0.906 whiteout)
foam on crests only, not flat areas
no zebra banding even in chop

vantages.desc: Ground level near Mt Fuji in a conifer forest, morning
vantages.expect: Dense green ground with real 3D conifer trees standing around the camera - visible brown trunks, needled canopies - and flatter silhouette-card trees carrying the forest toward the horizon. Grass underfoot.
regressions: 3D tree MODELS present, not just flat cards (v0.923 default-off regression)
no bare ground where a forest should be (bare-forest card-hide bug)
trunks visible, not floating canopies

vantages.desc: 400 km over the Sahara, the atmosphere limb in frame against stars
vantages.expect: The curved planet surface below a THIN bright blue atmosphere limb that fades smoothly into a black star-filled sky. Stars visible right down to the edge of the atmosphere band.
regressions: NO hard-edged opaque black shell/ring around the planet (v0.925 orbital occlusion bug)
the limb fades to stars, it does not cut off

vantages.desc: 12,000 km from Earth, the approved full-disc blue marble
vantages.expect: The full Earth disc as a blue marble with a soft blue atmosphere rim, oceans and continents visible, against stars. The operator-approved from-orbit look.
regressions: disc and rim look unchanged from the approved v0.918 baseline
no white-out, no missing atmosphere rim

vantages.desc: 200 m over the Moon's cratered surface, sunlit
vantages.expect: Continuous grey cratered lunar regolith with real crater bowls and rims and the true satellite-imagery albedo, under a black star sky. NOT bare flat facets.
regressions: real cratered terrain, NOT the bare uniform icosphere (v0.919 synthesized-heightmap fix)
no km-wide flat facets with hard creases

vantages.desc: Low altitude over water aimed at the setting sun
vantages.expect: A warm reddened sun low over water with a red/orange ember band along the horizon fading up into deepening blue and emerging stars. Sunlight visibly reddened.
regressions: sun disc reddened not white (v0.915 transmittance)
no washed dome, no black shell above the glow
